novice.name: Novice
novice.note: Just starting; learning the rules and etiquette

beginner.name: Beginner
beginner.note: 1-3 years playing; beginner level in casual drop-in games

lowerIntermediate.name: Lower Intermediate
lowerIntermediate.note: 3-5 years playing; lower-mid in casual drop-in games

intermediate.name: Intermediate
intermediate.note: 5-10 years playing; mid level in casual drop-in games

upperIntermediate.name: Upper Intermediate
upperIntermediate.note: 10+ years playing; upper-mid in casual drop-in games

advanced.name: Advanced
advanced.note: Front-line school team, sports-scholarship, amateur Division A, etc.

desc.1: Just picked up badminton; knows the match rules and court etiquette
desc.2: Can rally mid-high shots 10 strokes from mid-court; serves land over half the time
desc.3: From a fixed spot, half of rallies reach the back two-thirds of the court; serves land over 90% of the time
desc.4: Correct grip; from a fixed spot clears reach the back court (men) or mid-back (women); can do basic drives; solid on both forehand and backhand
desc.5: Correct grip; some basic footwork; handles standard shots reliably when not under pressure
desc.6: Basic footwork and rotation (not yet fluent); starting to smash and drop; when not pressured, clears on the move reach the mid-back court
desc.7: Smash, drop and clear-stationary or moving-land 70%+ consistently; has basic defense but little variation
desc.8: Has basic tactics and shot placement; comfortable with basic rotation; drops, smashes, clears and net shots 70%+ accurate; some defensive variation
desc.9: Three of drop/smash/clear/net shots are 90%+ accurate with quality; powerful strokes; defense has variation and consistency
desc.10: Rotation is second nature and used well; strategic play and placement score effectively
desc.11: Accurate on drop, smash, clear and net shots; combines power, speed and strategy; executes all backhand shots easily; threatening defense
desc.12: Fast movement and agile footwork; smashes, drops and net shots are fast and aggressive; often produces one-hit finishers
desc.13: All shots stable and polished; airtight defense, fast high-quality strokes, strong strategy and explosiveness
desc.14: All shots stable and polished; airtight defense, fast high-quality strokes (school team / sports-scholarship / amateur Division A level)
desc.15: All shots stable and polished; top amateur level
desc.16: Masterful shots, tactics and footwork; has developed a distinctive personal style
desc.17: Professional level; Division A / national representative player
